1.texto_original: ¶ Depois da morte de Acabe, Moab se rebelou contra Israel.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Reis 1:1

"¶ Depois da morte de Acabe, Moab se rebelou contra Israel."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Reis 1:1

Philological Analysis

"¶ Depois da morte de Acabe, Moab se rebelou contra Israel."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"¶ Depois da morte de Acabe, Moab se rebelou contra Israel."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

6.texto_original: Responderam-lhe eles: Um homem subiu ao nosso encontro, e nos disse: Ide, voltai para o rei que vos mandou, e dizei-lhe: Assim diz o Senhor: Porventura não há Deus em Israel, para que mandes consultar a Baal-Zebub, deus de Ecron? Portanto, da cama a que subiste não descerás, mas certamente morrerás.

13.texto_original: Ainda tornou o rei a enviar terceira vez um chefe de cinquenta com os seus cinquenta. E o terceiro chefe de cinquenta, subindo, veio e pôs-se de joelhos diante de Elias e suplicou-lhe, dizendo: ó homem de Deus, peço-te que seja preciosa aos teus olhos a minha vida, e a vida destes cinquenta teus servos.

🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Ainda tornou o rei a enviar terceira vez um chefe de cinquenta com os seus cinquenta. E o terceiro chefe de cinquenta, subindo, veio e pôs-se de joelhos diante de Elias e suplicou-lhe, dizendo: ó homem de Deus, peço-te que seja preciosa aos teus olhos a minha vida, e a vida destes cinquenta teus servos."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.texto_original: Eis que desceu fogo do céu, e consumiu aqueles dois primeiros chefes de cinquenta, com os seus cinquenta; agora, porém, seja preciosa aos teus olhos a minha vida.
14.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Reis 1:14

"Eis que desceu fogo do céu, e consumiu aqueles dois primeiros chefes de cinquenta, com os seus cinquenta; agora, porém, seja preciosa aos teus olhos a minha vida."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Reis 1:14

Philological Analysis

"Eis que desceu fogo do céu, e consumiu aqueles dois primeiros chefes de cinquenta, com os seus cinquenta; agora, porém, seja preciosa aos teus olhos a minha vida."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

16.texto_original: E disse-lhe: Assim diz o Senhor: Por que enviaste mensageiros a consultar a Baal-Zebub, deus de Ecron? Porventura é porque não há Deus em Israel, para consultares a sua palavra? Portanto, desta cama a que subiste não descerás, mas certamente morrerás.

17.texto_original: Assim, pois, morreu conforme a palavra do Senhor que Elias falara. E Jorão começou a reinar em seu lugar no ano segundo de Jorão, filho de Josafat, rei de Judá; porquanto Acazias não tinha filho.
17.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Reis 1:17

"Assim, pois, morreu conforme a palavra do Senhor que Elias falara. E Jorão começou a reinar em seu lugar no ano segundo de Jorão, filho de Josafat, rei de Judá; porquanto Acazias não tinha filho."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
